cards.name: Caffino Drive-Thru Espresso.
cards.address: 2790 Homestead Rd, Santa Clara, CA 95051, USA
cards.blurb: Quick drive-thru espresso to get the morning started — grab a latte and shake off the 52-degree chill before heading out.
cards.why: Good coffee fast, so you're not dragging when the art show opens.

cards.name: Jonathan Calm - To Wherever, Forever: Archives of Absence & Sites of Passage
cards.blurb: Explore two linked exhibitions examining absence, memory, and migration through art.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a genuinely good way to spend a cool Tuesday morning.

cards.name: Iraklis Restaurant
cards.address: 2165 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Mediterranean spot on El Camino with serious value and solid food. Try whatever's fresh off the grill and settle in.
cards.why: It's the kind of place locals go back to repeatedly for a reason.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Free contemporary art museum with a sculpture garden worth wandering even on a gray day. The indoor galleries are strong, and the outdoor sculptures hold up in cooler weather.
cards.why: The Triton is genuinely underrated and this is a great low-key afternoon anchor.

cards.name: Portuguese Tasty Desserts
cards.address: 1085 El Camino Real, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Portuguese bakery with a knockout reputation. Grab a pastel de nata or whatever's left in the case before they close at 4.
cards.why: A 4.8 rating at a bakery is basically a mandate to stop in.

cards.name: Xiang Xiang Noodle
cards.address: 1115 Tasman Dr #2228, Sunnyvale, CA 94089, USA
cards.blurb: Slurp your way through a bowl of hand-pulled noodles in a warm spot after a full afternoon out. The broth is the move here.
cards.why: Exactly what you want on a cool evening after a day of walking around.

cards.name: West Valley College Aquatics Center
cards.address: Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a swim at West Valley College's aquatics center — lap lanes, cool air, and a clean facility to wake up properly before the day kicks in.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make an indoor pool feel like the smartest possible call.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: You practice English speaking and listening while learning about other cultures in conversation.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Perrucci Family Vineyard
cards.address: 15050 Kennedy Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Perrucci Family Vineyard for a midday pour in a relaxed, intimate outdoor setting — this is a small family operation with a genuinely warm vibe.
cards.why: A Tuesday afternoon at a low-key vineyard hits different when you're not fighting weekend crowds.

cards.name: Cloud 9 Foot Spa San Jose - Formerly Oceanic
cards.address: 2989 Union Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Book a foot spa session at Cloud 9 and let someone work out whatever the morning hike left behind — it's a solid hour of doing absolutely nothing productive and that's the point.
cards.why: After a trail walk and a wine stop, this is exactly the right gear shift.
cards.venue: Cloud 9 Foot Spa San Jose - Formerly Oceanic

cards.name: Heritage Pub and Chop House
cards.address: 15 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at Heritage Pub and Chop House on Santa Cruz Ave — settle into a booth, order something hearty, and take your time with it.
cards.why: It's a solid sit-down spot right in the heart of Los Gatos for a Tuesday night wind-down.

cards.name: Montalvo Arts Center Arboretum
cards.address: Montalvo Arts Center
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a stroll through Montalvo's peaceful arboretum trails, surrounded by towering redwoods and sculpted gardens that feel worlds away from the city.
cards.why: A calm, close-to-home outdoor finish keeps the evening easy after dinner in Los Gatos.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Triton Museum of Art, wandering the contemporary galleries and then stepping into the sculpture garden — the outdoor space is beautiful even on a cool morning.
cards.why: Free world-class art in Santa Clara that most people don't even know exists.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail for a relaxed post-museum reset. The path follows the creek through shade trees — flat, easy, and genuinely pretty.
cards.why: Good legs-and-fresh-air break before the afternoon stretch.

cards.name: Gali Vineyards
cards.address: 21 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Gali Vineyards opens at 3 PM right on Main Street in Los Gatos. Grab a glass, settle in, and let the afternoon slow down a bit.
cards.why: Perfect spot to decompress and enjoy a pour after being on your feet.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Dio Deka for dinner. It's the best Greek food in Los Gatos — the patio is worth requesting, and you'll want to linger over the menu.
cards.why: A dinner worth dressing up a little for, and the patio makes it feel like a real evening out.

cards.name: GOLF SQUARE SAN JOSE
cards.address: 2590 S Bascom Ave Suite B, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Get an early tee time at this indoor golf simulator and warm up the morning with a few swings before the crowds show up.
cards.why: It's a cool morning and hitting a simulator beats standing outside in 52 degrees.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the full sweep of computing history, from room-sized mainframes to the personal computer revolution — the exhibits are genuinely wild and the artifacts are real.
cards.why: Two hours in here goes fast, and you'll leave knowing something you didn't before.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the outdoor plaza at Main Street Cupertino and grab lunch at one of the restaurants lining the strip, with Apple Park sitting right in the backdrop.
cards.why: It's the most pleasant afternoon spot in Cupertino and lunch options here actually cover a lot of ground.

cards.name: J. Lohr Vineyards & Wines
cards.address: 1000 Lenzen Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for a tasting at J. Lohr's San Jose winery, working through their reds and whites in a relaxed, no-rush setting with knowledgeable pourers.
cards.why: A Wednesday afternoon at a winery with a 4.9 reputation is exactly what midweek is for.

cards.name: Roja Los Altos
cards.address: 242 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the evening at Roja Los Altos with a cocktail or glass of wine and something from their dinner menu to ease into the night.
cards.why: The vibe here matches the pace of a Wednesday evening perfectly.

cards.name: Grant Park
cards.address: 1575 Holt Ave, Los Altos, CA 94024, USA
cards.blurb: Nice spot to get some air.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: End the night with a few frames at the SJSU bowling center, which stays open late and has a laid-back weeknight crowd.
cards.why: Bowling is always a good call when you want to keep the night going without overthinking it.

cards.name: TrioSpa - Massage, Facials & Waxing / Trio Wellness Mgmt
cards.address: 1585 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a massage or facial at TrioSpa. It's a legit spa morning — book whatever sounds good and let them take care of you for an hour.
cards.why: Cool morning, nowhere to be yet — this is exactly when a spa visit makes sense.
cards.venue: TrioSpa - Massage, Facials & Waxing / Trio Wellness Mgmt

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the Triton's galleries and sculpture garden. The contemporary art rotates regularly and the outdoor garden is worth taking your time in.
cards.why: Free admission and genuinely good art — hard to beat that for a Tuesday morning.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market and graze your way through lunch. Grab tacos from one vendor, something from another, find a patio seat and make an afternoon of it.
cards.why: One of the best lunch spots in downtown San Jose — the variety means you'll both find something you actually want.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Wander the creekside trails and open meadows at Alum Rock Park, a great spot to stretch your legs among eucalyptus groves and natural mineral springs.
cards.why: A relaxed afternoon outdoors is a nice reset between lunch and the evening entertainment.

cards.name: Mother's Day Craft: Cardmaking
cards.address: 290 International Circle San Jose
cards.blurb: Design and create a handmade card celebrating your mother or mother figure with your own personal touch.
cards.why: It's a low-key, creative way to spend the early evening before heading out for the Irish session.

cards.name: Live Irish Seisiún
cards.address: 25 N San Pedro St., San Jose
cards.blurb: Experience traditional Irish music sessions at O Flaherty's Irish Pub.
cards.why: Traditional Irish sessions are rare and genuinely fun — this one is worth the trip downtown.

cards.name: Ridge Vineyards
cards.address: 17100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Drive up Montebello Road and taste through Ridge's lineup on the hilltop terrace. The Zinfandels and Cabernets are the main event, and the views over the valley are worth the winding road up.
cards.why: A weekday morning tasting means a quieter experience and more time with the staff.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the outdoor plaza, pick a spot from one of the Asian restaurants or grab a seat at Lazy Dog, and take your time with lunch in the open air.
cards.why: It's the most relaxed midday stop in Cupertino and a good reset after wine tasting.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the lakeside trails, watch the ducks, and decompress between a big lunch and an evening out in Los Gatos — the park is right there when you need a breath of fresh air.
cards.why: It bridges the afternoon gap perfectly and keeps you in the Los Gatos area ahead of dinner at Dio Deka.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Snag a patio table at Dio Deka and start with drinks and a few mezze before dinner proper. The space is beautiful and the food matches it — dress up a little and take your time.
cards.why: It's the best dinner in Los Gatos and a perfect way to end the day on a high note.

cards.name: Trivia Night
cards.address: 25 N San Pedro St., San Jose
cards.blurb: Challenge yourself and friends to a trivia competition at the pub.
cards.why: A Wednesday trivia night is exactly the kind of low-effort, high-fun way to close out the evening.

cards.name: Monday Morning Meditation & Mindfulness
cards.blurb: Sit for a 20-minute guided meditation session, then ask questions and discuss with instructor Manisha.
cards.why: Free, chill, and a solid way to start a Monday with intention.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the creek trail at your own pace. It's shaded and flat, so you can go as far or as short as you want before doubling back.
cards.why: Cool morning temps make this the perfect window for a walk outside.

cards.name: Bowlero Valley Fair
cards.address: 2855 Stevens Creek Blvd #1005, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a lane and knock out a few games. It's a solid afternoon escape when you want to be active without being outdoors.
cards.why: Bowling is criminally underrated for a weekday afternoon with adults.

cards.name: Cooper-Garrod Vineyards at Garrod Farms
cards.address: 22645 Garrod Rd, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Taste through their estate wines on the outdoor patio at Garrod Farms. The setting is relaxed and unpretentious, and the vineyard views don't hurt.
cards.why: This is exactly the kind of low-key winery afternoon that's hard to beat on a mild Monday.

cards.name: GOLF SQUARE SAN JOSE
cards.address: 2590 S Bascom Ave Suite B, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Step into a bay of golf simulators and work on your swing with realistic course play and instant shot feedback.
cards.why: A fun, low-key way to burn some time between the vineyard and your evening shopping stop.

cards.name: Mai Do - Fine Stationery & Goods - Santana Row
cards.address: 378 Santana Row #1125, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the fine stationery, pens, and goods at Mai Do on Santana Row. It's the kind of shop where you go in for a card and leave with three things you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: Santana Row in the evening is a great vibe, and Mai Do is one of the more distinctive shops on the strip.
cards.venue: Mai Do - Fine Stationery & Goods - Santana Row

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — the exhibits on the origins of computing, the internet, and early software are genuinely fascinating even if you've never written a line of code.
cards.why: Cool and overcast is the perfect excuse to go deep on a museum you've probably been meaning to visit forever.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up to Picchetti Winery for a glass and some air — the outdoor setting is beautiful and the wines are solid, so grab a pour and sit outside if the clouds cooperate.
cards.why: A winery lunch beats a restaurant any Wednesday.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk the trails around Shoreline Lake, watch the windsurfers, or just wander the open bay flats and decompress for a couple hours.
cards.why: The fresh air after a winery visit is basically mandatory.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll the open-air corridor at Main Street Cupertino for a coffee, light bite, or browse through its mix of local shops and casual dining spots.
cards.why: It's a natural wind-down stop right after Shoreline Park and keeps you loosely in the west-side orbit before heading east to dinner.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Smoking Pig BBQ — get the brisket plate, add the mac and cheese, and don't overthink it.
cards.why: It's the best BBQ in the South Bay and you've earned a real meal.
